title: T-101 — Dept Taxonomy Editor (SET-061) — split/merge/add wizard (ADR-030)
prompt:
# T-101 — Dept Taxonomy Editor (SET-061) — split/merge/add wizard (ADR-030)

## Goal
Build SET-061 (prototype: none (UX 02-SETTINGS-UX.md §dept-taxonomy)) following prototype `design/Monopilot Design System/settings/modals.jsx:262-375` 1:1 plus the production contract notes below.

Wizard for split/merge/add dept actions. Includes column_mapping picker for split. Calls T-027 setDeptOverrides.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/tenant/dept-taxonomy/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/tenant/dept-taxonomy/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the production page renders, when compared to `design/Monopilot Design System/settings/modals.jsx:262-375` (prototype: none (UX 02-SETTINGS-UX.md §dept-taxonomy)), then it has structural parity (the same form sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives, same modals invoked from same triggers), visual parity (same shadcn/Radix primitives — no drift to plain HTML, same density tokens), and interaction parity (same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order) — verified by an RTL snapshot test plus the parity checklist embedded in `pipeline_inputs.details`.
- Given action='split' source='technical' targets=['food-safety','quality-lab'] without column_mapping, when submitted, then UI raises COLUMN_MAPPING_REQUIRED (V-SET-30).
- Given action='add' new code='regulatory-affairs', when submitted, then tenant_variations.dept_overrides JSONB receives the new entry.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Add a failing test that exercises the acceptance criteria above. The RED command is:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/tenant/dept-taxonomy/page.test.tsx [create]`
2. Run RED — it must fail before any production code is written.
3. Implement the smallest change needed for GREEN.
4. Re-run RED command — must pass.
5. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not edit files outside the listed scope without documenting why in CLOSEOUT.
- Do not weaken existing acceptance criteria from prior tasks.
- Do not introduce REST endpoints; use Server Actions per stack contract.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED→GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. Primitives in `packages/ui`. PRD: `02-SETTINGS-PRD.md` v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.details:
Prototype: design/Monopilot Design System/settings/modals.jsx:262-375. Translation notes (canonical from prototype-index-settings.json): see `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-settings.md` and the entry's `translation_notes` array. Production contract: data via Server Components (Drizzle); mutations via Server Actions; primitives from `packages/ui` only (no @radix-ui/react-dialog import outside packages/ui per §0). Parity checklist (verify item-by-item before GREEN):
1. Section/region count + order matches prototype.
2. Field labels + types match prototype.
3. shadcn/Radix primitives match (Input, Select, Switch, Dialog etc).
4. Action buttons present in same order with same enable/disable rules.
5. Loading/empty/error states render per prototype.
6. Modal triggers open the correct modal id (SM-NN).
7. Keyboard focus order matches prototype.
8. assertModalA11y() passes for any modals invoked.
acceptance_criteria: Given the production page renders, when compared to `design/Monopilot Design System/settings/modals.jsx:262-375` (prototype: none (UX 02-SETTINGS-UX.md §dept-taxonomy)), then it has structural parity (the same form sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives, same modals invoked from same triggers), visual parity (same shadcn/Radix primitives — no drift to plain HTML, same density tokens), and interaction parity (same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order) — verified by an RTL snapshot test plus the parity checklist embedded in `pipeline_inputs.details`.
Given action='split' source='technical' targets=['food-safety','quality-lab'] without column_mapping, when submitted, then UI raises COLUMN_MAPPING_REQUIRED (V-SET-30).
Given action='add' new code='regulatory-affairs', when submitted, then tenant_variations.dept_overrides JSONB receives the new entry.
test_strategy: RED: write RTL test that mounts the page with mocked data and asserts the parity AC items + ac_extra coverage.
GREEN: build component, wire Server Action(s).
risk_red_lines: Do not import @radix-ui/react-dialog outside packages/ui (§0 ESLint rule).
Do not paste prototype JSX wholesale — translate to production contract.
Do not regress accessibility (axe violations = fail).
